principle.title: Photograph the rental before and after — security-deposit deductions now depend on it
principle.plainRule: For California residential rentals, landlords must document unit condition with photographs at required stages and provide supporting photographs with qualifying security-deposit deductions. The remaining deposit and itemized accounting are generally due within 21 calendar days after the tenant vacates.
principle.globalBasis: California Civil Code section 1950.5, as amended by AB 2801
principle.globalSourceTitle: California Legislature — AB 2801 chaptered bill text
principle.secondaryGlobalSourceTitle: California Courts — Guide to security deposits
principle.everydayMeaning: A landlord should not rely only on a written checklist or memory. For tenancies beginning on or after July 1, 2025, take clear move-in photographs immediately before or at the start of the tenancy. After possession is returned, photograph the unit before any cleaning or repair for which a deduction may be claimed, and photograph it again after the work is completed. Keep invoices, receipts, labor details and timestamps together with the photos.
practicalSteps: Create a dated room-by-room photo set at move-in, including floors, walls, appliances, bathrooms and any existing damage.
When the tenant moves out, photograph the unit before cleaning or repairs and preserve the original files and timestamps.
Photograph the completed repair or cleaning, and match each claimed deduction to the relevant before-and-after images.
Send the itemized statement, required supporting documents, photographs and any remaining deposit within the applicable 21-day period.
principle.incidentTitle: California enacted AB 2801 to strengthen security-deposit documentation
principle.incidentSummary: California amended Civil Code section 1950.5 after concerns that security deposits were being used for upgrades or charges not adequately tied to tenant-caused damage. The law added mandatory photographic documentation and clarified that deductions must restore the unit to its starting condition, excluding ordinary wear and tear.
principle.incidentOutcome: Move-out photo requirements began April 1, 2025, and move-in photo requirements apply to tenancies beginning on or after July 1, 2025. Bad-faith failure to comply can prevent a landlord from claiming the deposit and may expose the landlord to additional damages.
principle.takeaway: Treat security-deposit deductions like an evidence file: dated move-in photos, move-out photos, post-work photos, receipts and a timely itemized statement should all tell the same story.
principle.localLawWarning: This entry summarizes California state law only. Local rent-control rules, lease terms, subsidized-housing requirements and the facts of a particular tenancy may add obligations. Landlords should use current California forms and obtain legal advice before withholding a disputed deposit.

site.scopeNote: Today’s entry is California-specific because security-deposit procedures differ by state and country. It is general legal education, not advice for a particular tenancy.
